protocol.name: nixbench_html_theme_v1
protocol.copyright:
Copyright 2026 the NixBench contributors
Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the conditions of the NixBench BSD 2-Clause license are met.

interface.name: nixbench_html_theme_manager_v1

description.summary: private NixBench HTML chrome renderer registration

interface.description:
This private protocol connects the ordinary-user NixBench desktop core to the single ordinary-user HTML theme renderer launched for its session. It is not an application integration protocol.
Registration requires the unguessable token inherited by the renderer. A registered wl_surface is a browser-produced atlas carrier. This interface records private metadata but does not assign a wl_surface role; toolkit-created xdg-shell state may coexist with it. The compositor does not map the registered surface as an application window.

entry.name: invalid_token
entry.summary: renderer registration token did not match

entry.name: already_registered
entry.summary: the session already has an atlas renderer

entry.name: invalid_surface
entry.summary: surface is already buffered or privately assigned

request.name: register_atlas
request.description: Register surface as the session's HTML chrome atlas. The token must match the token retained by the desktop core. Registration must occur before the surface's first non-null buffer attachment. Toolkit setup commits which do not attach pixels are allowed.

interface.name: nixbench_html_theme_atlas_v1

description.summary: HTML chrome atlas state and layout

interface.description:
The desktop core publishes immutable shell state in serial-numbered transactions. The renderer lays out named tiles in one transparent atlas and returns tile and action rectangles for a generation. Atlas pixels are supplied by committing a buffer to the registered wl_surface.
The core composes every tile independently. Renderer coordinates never become authoritative window geometry or input policy.

request.name: ack_state
request.description: The renderer has applied state serial to its DOM. This does not imply that the corresponding pixels have been painted.

request.name: begin_layout

description.summary: begin atlas metadata for a pixel generation

request.description: Begin replacing all tile and action metadata. Width and height describe the browser buffer which will be committed for generation.

request.name: tile

description.summary: publish one independently composited atlas tile

request.description: For window tiles object_hi/object_lo contain the NixBench window id. They are zero for singleton desktop and menubar tiles.

request.name: action_region
request.description: Coordinates are relative to the corresponding tile. The compositor treats these as presentation hints and applies its own geometry and window-state validation before executing an action.

request.name: commit_layout

description.summary: complete atlas metadata for a generation

request.description: The layout becomes eligible only after a matching wl_surface buffer commit has also been accepted. A generation replaces the previous layout atomically.

event.name: configure

description.summary: configure atlas limits and output scale

event.description: Atlas dimensions are upper bounds. The renderer may use a smaller buffer. Serial is acknowledged after the renderer applies the complete state transaction ending in state_done.

event.name: theme

description.summary: select one validated local theme bundle

event.description: Directory is the canonical root validated by the desktop core. Theme id is supplied separately for DOM state and diagnostics.

event.name: window
event.description: Width and height describe the complete decorated frame requested by the compositor. State is a bitfield of window_state values.
